Off Calendar Mar-27-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Feb-28-2024 Continued To Mar-27-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
John Coward, Esq., for Sally Williams, Petitioner.

At the outset, Mr. Coward offers the court an update on the matter and reports that there is a deposition set for next week and that he is considering seeking a judgment. Counsel reports that efforts to mediate have been unsuccessful so far.

After additional discussion, the subject matters are continued to Mar-27-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Dec-04-2023 Continued To Feb-28-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

John H. Coward, Esq., for Sally Williams, Personal Representative.
Dominic Redono, Esq., for Joseph Covello, Objector.

Counsel presented updates regarding the status of mediation. Mr. Rodono confirms his client's commitment to mediate but that he has found it difficult to schedule a mediation due to his client's age.

After additional discussion, the subject matters are continued to Feb-28-2024 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court advises counsel that it will be issuing an amended trial order as the original trial order previously issued by the court has been updated and the new trial order includes language regarding the parties scheduling a Mandatory Settlement Conference through the court's ADR Department. The court confirms that the trial date remains as set. Counsel is advised to monitor the court's website to obtain a copy of the amended order for details.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Pliego/Singh. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-28-2023 Continued To Dec-04-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Coward appeared by zoom for Sally Williams.
Dominic Rodono appeared by zoom for Joseph Covello.

Discussion was held regarding the difference in opinion with respect to the length of trial, the need to conduct discovery, whether the parties are interested in doing mediation, the pending conservatorship action in Kern County, whether it makes sense to set a trial date now or wait until after discovery is concluded, etc. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than November 20, 2023. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by November 27, 2023. Status of mediation is set for December 4, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The case management conference is also continued to December 4, 2023. The Petition to Confirm Estate Assets, etc. is set for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on May 13, 2024 at 9:30 a.m., (order for the same signed in open Court). The parties are to monitor the court's website and obtain copies of the mediation order and the trial order.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Sae Phan. Not reported.

Petition To (1) Confirm Estate Assets, (2) Financial Elder Abuse, (3) Breach Of Fiduciary Duty, And (4) Undue Influence
Off Calendar Aug-28-2023 Continued To May-13-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Petition To (1) Confirm Estate Assets, (2) Financial Elder Abuse, (3) Breach Of Fiduciary Duty, And (4) Undue Influence
Off Calendar Jun-26-2023 Continued To Aug-28-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Coward, via Zoom, for Sally Williams, Petitioner; Dominic Rodono, for Joseph Covello, Respondent.

Mr. Rodono confirms that while Eugene Covello's health may be an issue, he retains capacity. Mr. Coward requests an evidentiary hearing in six to eight months and advises that the hearing may take two days. Mr. Rodono confirms that up to now, there has been no discovery and believes that having an evidentiary hearing in six to eight months might be premature. The court offers its position and suggests having a Case Management Hearing in sixty days and the court summarizes the process leading up to possibly setting trial. Counsel agrees with the court's suggestion and is advised that the court shall issue an order RE: Case Management.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Aug-28-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. A Case Management Hearing is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Petition To (1) Confirm Estate Assets, (2) Financial Elder Abuse, (3) Breach Of Fiduciary Duty, And (4) Undue Influence
Off Calendar Mar-06-2023 Continued To Jun-26-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Coward, via Zoom, for Sally J. Williams, Personal Representative; Dominic Rodono, via Zoom, for Joseph Covello, interested Party.

The court notes the service on Joseph Covello upon his caretaker. The court confirms with Mr. Rodono that his client is in his nineties and of his intention to file either an objection or a motion and requests a continuance to do so. Mr. Coward does not object to Mr. Rodono's request so long as the court sets a deadline for the filing of an opposition or demurrer. The court and counsel discuss the preceding and counsel agree to a continuance.

The matter is continued to Jun-26-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. An objection or a demurrer must be filed by May-22-2023 and a response to any objection must be filed by Jun-05-2023.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-18-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Randall Boessenecker for Sally Williams.

Mr. Boessenecker stated that all of the property is separate property of the Decedent, that one quarter of the rental income is the Decedent's and is referenced in the Trust, that he has the consent of the Decedent's Widow for the reimbursement to the Petitioner and that if it is not acceptable to the Court, the Petitioner can pay it back. The Court instructed Mr. Boessenecker to provide/file a copy of the trust and an amendment. The Final Report and Petition for Settlement of Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-31-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Randy Boessenecker for Sally Williams.

The Court stated that it read the pleadings and that there is sufficient evidence to overcome the presumption of revocation. The will is admitted to probate. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond is waived. Status on close of estate is set for February 27, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
